publisher.name: TensorFeed.ai
publisher.description: Real-time AI news, model pricing, service status, and machine-payable premium intelligence for AI agents.
publisher.agent_fair_trade: TensorFeed.ai is agent fair-trade certified: open pricing, automatic no-charge on 5xx, breaker, schema fail, and stale data, Ed25519-signed receipts on every paid call, inference-only license. Built with Claude (Anthropic). Standard at /.well-known/agent-fair-trade.json.

live.note: USDC on Base mainnet, verified via Base RPC

evaluating.note: Stripe Link Shared Payment Tokens via x402 with method=stripe in www-authenticate. Under evaluation; not yet accepted.

operator.note: Pizza Robot Studios LLC is the legal entity behind the payment wallet. Premium credits are non-refundable per Section 17.5 of the Terms of Service.

validation.note: Cross-check the wallet address across all four published locations before sending funds. If any disagree, do not send.

welcomeBonus.trigger: first successful USDC payment from a new sender wallet

credits.description: Recommended for repeat use. Buy a bearer token once, decrement credits per call.

x402.description: Canonical Coinbase x402 V2 exact scheme on Base mainnet. Call any /api/premium/* endpoint without auth to receive a 402 with PaymentRequired body. Sign an EIP-3009 transferWithAuthorization for the exact amount, base64-encode the PaymentPayload, retry with the X-PAYMENT header. The TensorFeed facilitator broadcasts on-chain and returns 200 with PAYMENT-RESPONSE settlement receipt and a credits token. Compatible with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Payments and the @coinbase/x402 SDK out of the box.
x402.verification: Facilitator verifies EIP-712 signature against USDC v2 domain on Base mainnet (chainId 8453, contract 0x833589fCD6eDb6E08f4c7C32D4f71b54bdA02913), checks parameter alignment with PaymentRequirements, time window, and EIP-3009 nonce state, then broadcasts via USDC.transferWithAuthorization. Replay protection: USDC enforces (from, nonce) uniqueness on-chain; facilitator also stakes a KV claim before broadcast.

x402_legacy.description: TensorFeed-specific legacy fallback. The agent pre-broadcasts USDC on Base, then submits the tx hash plus a quote nonce. Kept for back-compat with TensorFeed SDK clients shipped before the standards-compliant facilitator. Vanilla Coinbase x402 clients including AgentCore Payments should use the X-PAYMENT path above.
x402_legacy.verification: Worker reads the USDC Transfer event from eth_getTransactionReceipt on the Base mainnet RPC. Replay protection: every used tx hash is permanently recorded. AFTA Tx-Sniper guard binds the on-chain sender to the X-Payment-Quote nonce.

terms.noTraining: Premium data is licensed for inference use only. Use of TensorFeed premium endpoint responses for training, fine-tuning, evaluation, or distillation of machine learning models is prohibited.
terms.refund: All credit purchases are final and non-refundable per Section 17.5 of the Terms. Credits do not expire and remain redeemable across tensorfeed.ai and terminalfeed.io. Recommended pattern: buy small (for example, $1 USDC for 50 credits), calibrate, top up as needed.
terms.sla: No SLA in MVP phase. Credits do not expire.
terms.sanctions: Premium API access is unavailable to persons or entities subject to OFAC, EU, UK, or UN sanctions, and to residents of comprehensively sanctioned jurisdictions (Cuba, Iran, North Korea, Syria, Crimea, Donetsk, Luhansk). Inbound credit-purchase transactions are screened against the Chainalysis public sanctions API. See https://tensorfeed.ai/terms#premium Section 17.9.
terms.acceptableUse: No reselling of bearer tokens or proxy APIs that materially reproduce the Premium API surface. No use of premium responses to train or evaluate competing models. See Section 17.12.
terms.governingLaw: California law. Exclusive venue: Los Angeles County, California. See Section 15 Governing Law and Venue.

metadata.name: Premium routing recommendations
metadata.description: Top-N ranked AI model recommendations for a task with full composite-score breakdown across quality, availability, cost, and latency. Synthesizes live pricing, benchmarks, and provider status.

task.description: Task family the model needs to be good at

metadata.name: Pricing series
metadata.description: Daily input/output/blended price points for one model with min/max/delta summary. Default 30-day window, max 90 days.

metadata.name: Benchmark score series
metadata.description: Score evolution for a single benchmark on one model with delta in percentage points. Supported keys: swe_bench, mmlu_pro, gpqa_diamond, math, human_eval.

metadata.name: Uptime rollup
metadata.description: Daily uptime rollup for one provider with operational, degraded, and down day counts. Uptime % treats degraded as half-credit. Missing-data days excluded from denominator.

metadata.name: Full news history snapshot
metadata.description: Untruncated TensorFeed daily news archive. Pass ?date=YYYY-MM-DD for one day, or ?from=&to= for a range up to 30 days. Each day returns up to 200 deduped articles captured from the most recent hourly RSS poll.

metadata.name: RSS source-health series
metadata.description: Multi-day per-source RSS poll reliability series across all TensorFeed news sources. Daily polls/poll_ok/poll_empty/poll_error/articles_total per source, plus reliability_pct and last_status. Range capped at 90 days.

metadata.name: CVE range query
metadata.description: MITRE CVE List range query: CVE IDs added or modified across a UTC date range, capped at 30 days. Each day in the response carries the full CVE-ID set indexed by the daily cvelistV5 commit-history scan. Pair with /api/security/cve/{id} for the per-CVE Record v5.2 lookup. License: MITRE CVE Terms of Use, commercial redistribution permitted; attribution block included on every response.

metadata.name: CISA KEV full catalog
metadata.description: Full untruncated CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. Free tier returns top-50 most recent entries; this endpoint returns the entire current catalog (~1500+ entries) with full entry detail. License: US Government public domain (17 USC 105), commercial redistribution explicitly permitted; attribution block on every response.

metadata.name: CISA KEV added series
metadata.description: Multi-day series of CISA KEV catalog additions across a UTC date range, capped at 90 days. Each day returns the full entry list whose dateAdded fell on that day. License: US Government public domain (17 USC 105), commercial redistribution explicitly permitted.

metadata.name: EPSS time series
metadata.description: Full historical EPSS (Exploit Prediction Scoring System) time-series for one CVE, sourced from FIRST.org. EPSS estimates the daily probability of exploitation in the next 30 days. License: free for any use per FIRST.org policy; commercial redistribution permitted.

metadata.name: EPSS top-N
metadata.description: Top-N CVEs ranked by current or historical EPSS exploitation-likelihood score. Optional ?date=YYYY-MM-DD returns the leaderboard as of any past date. License: FIRST.org free-for-any-use policy.

metadata.name: NASA POWER hourly meteorological + solar
metadata.description: Hourly-resolution meteorological and solar data for one point, sourced from NASA Langley's POWER API. Same source as free /api/climate/power/daily; hourly bins produce ~24x payload so range is capped at 30 days. Parameters include T2M, RH2M, PRECTOTCORR, WS10M, ALLSKY_SFC_SW_DWN, and 100+ others. License: NASA POWER open access; US Government work in the public domain.

metadata.name: OpenFDA aggregate query
metadata.description: Histogram-by-field across openFDA's drug, food, and device record sets (FAERS, MAUDE, structured product labels, enforcement reports). Uses openFDA's `count` parameter to return bucket counts in one call instead of paging through millions of individual records. Useful for top-N analyses (top drugs by adverse events, top reactions for one drug, top recalled food categories). License: CC0 1.0 Universal Dedication, FDA waiver of all copyright interests, commercial redistribution permitted.

metadata.name: LLM-ready CVE record
metadata.description: MITRE CVE Record v5.2 transformed into a flat token-efficient payload optimized for LLM context windows. Drops typical record from ~3KB nested JSON to ~500 bytes flat JSON (~80% token reduction) with no information loss for agent decision-making. Adds derived severity_band, deduped CWEs, flat affected_products, top references. Versioned via schema_version + cleaning_version for stability. License: MITRE CVE Terms of Use; commercial redistribution permitted.

metadata.name: LLM-ready KEV entry
metadata.description: CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities entry transformed into LLM-ready format with normalized ransomware_use enum (yes / unknown / no) and extracted notes_urls. Versioned for stability. License: US Government public domain.

metadata.name: LLM-ready EPSS score
metadata.description: FIRST.org EPSS score transformed into LLM-ready format with numeric probability (not stringified), derived risk_band (low/medium/high/critical), and optional series summary (first/min/max points instead of full daily series). Optional ?series=true returns the summary; default returns the current point only. License: FIRST.org free-for-any-use policy.

metadata.name: Verified CVE (cross-database)
metadata.description: Single-call cross-database CVE verification. Composes MITRE CVE Record + CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities + FIRST.org EPSS + OSV.dev advisory + CISA Vulnrichment SSVC into one LLM-ready fact card with confirmed_by array and corroboration_count. Reduces 5 fan-out calls and 5 different parsing schemas to one. The anti-hallucination lookup for security agents: corroboration_count is auditable per call. Sources missing the CVE just don't appear in confirmed_by; the call still returns whatever sources do have it. License terms inherited from each upstream source; attribution block per source included on every response.

metadata.name: LLM-ready OpenRouter model card
metadata.description: One model from the OpenRouter catalog (367+ entries) returned as an LLM-ready fact card. Pricing normalized to USD per million tokens with derived blended_5_to_1 input:output mix. Capability flags (tools, vision, structured_outputs, reasoning, response_format) extracted from supported_parameters + modality so agents can boolean-filter without parsing the raw parameter array. Free-tier detection (is_free_tier) and creation-date ISO normalization included. Compression typically 99%+ (270KB catalog -> ~500B card) since OpenRouter has no per-id filter on its public catalog endpoint. License: Public catalog data; pricing and capabilities owned by OpenRouter and the underlying inference providers.

metadata.name: LLM-ready NASA POWER daily
metadata.description: NASA POWER point query transformed into LLM-ready format. Pivots NASA's parameter-keyed dicts into date-keyed rows for token-efficient agent consumption. NASA fill value (-999) becomes null; date strings normalized to ISO 8601. Same parameters and same data as free /api/climate/power/daily, just shaped for direct LLM ingestion. License: US Government public domain.

metadata.name: LLM-ready EIA series
metadata.description: EIA Open Data series transformed into LLM-ready format. Sorts ascending, extracts numeric values from stringified upstream, surfaces primary_units, and computes MoM and YoY delta percentages against valid (non-null) observations. The deltas are usually the agent's whole reason for asking; we save them the math and the parsing. License: US Government public domain.

metadata.name: News story clusters (full)
metadata.description: Full untruncated story-level cross-source corroboration clusters per UTC date or range up to 30 days. Each cluster groups TensorFeed news articles about the same story via embedding-based similarity (Workers AI bge-base-en-v1.5 at threshold 0.82) and reports source_count, sources_list, hero article (earliest publishedAt), corroboration_band (single/limited/broad), and contributing article_ids. The free /api/history/news/clusters caps at 25 clusters/day; this endpoint returns all clusters with no cap.

metadata.name: Verified news feed
metadata.description: Story-level news feed filtered to clusters with N or more independent sources corroborating. Default min_sources=4 (broad corroboration). Agents asking 'do not act on a single source' get a clean stream of stories that cleared the trust threshold. The trust play Gemini named: only TF can ship this because only TF has the cross-source view at scale. Single-date or 30-day range. Computed nightly via Workers AI bge-base-en-v1.5 embedding clustering.

metadata.name: LLM-ready OpenFDA query
metadata.description: OpenFDA query results transformed into LLM-ready format with per-category flat schemas. drug/events flattens FAERS adverse-event reports with patient demo + drugs + reactions + outcomes + seriousness flags. drug/labels surfaces brand/generic/manufacturer + section text. drug/recalls + food/recalls expose enforcement reports with classification + reason + voluntary flag. device/events extracts primary device + outcomes + truncated narrative from MAUDE. License: CC0 1.0 Universal Dedication.

metadata.name: Enriched agents directory
metadata.description: Static AI agents catalog joined with live status, recent news count and top 3 articles, agent traffic, flagship pricing, and a derived 0-100 trending_score. Server-side filter and sort.

metadata.name: News search
metadata.description: Full-text search over the AI news corpus with relevance scoring (term hits weighted 3 in title, 1 in snippet, plus recency boost) and date/provider/category filters.

metadata.name: What's new
metadata.description: Agent morning brief: pricing changes, new/removed models, status incidents, and top news headlines from the last 1-7 days. The endpoint to call when an agent boots up.

metadata.name: Macro digest
metadata.description: Joins free BLS and FRED snapshots into one agent-shaped morning brief: rates section with yield-curve regime classification, inflation section with regime classification, employment section with regime classification, growth + money, FX + commodities, plus templated headline narratives per section and a 2-3 sentence overall brief. Compute is the gate; raw upstream data remains free.

metadata.name: AI policy timeline
metadata.description: Forward + backward calendar over the free /api/policy/ai/registry catalog. Each entry classified relative to now (past / today / upcoming / far-future), with days-until-effective. Configurable window (default 12 months: 6 back, 6 forward, 5-year cap). Surfaces next 3 upcoming milestones, aggregates by jurisdiction, and ships every entry with its source URL preserved. Compute is the gate; raw registry remains free.

metadata.name: Recession watch
metadata.description: Composite recession-risk signal across two well-known indicators: yield-curve inversion (FRED 10Y-2Y spread) and the Sahm rule (current 3-month unemployment moving avg vs trailing-12-month low). Per-signal red/yellow/green classification with editorial explanation, plus a composite signal (red if either is red, green only if both green, yellow otherwise) and a brief synthesis paragraph. Distinct from the macro digest: this endpoint takes a stance on recession risk rather than serving the broad snapshot.

metadata.name: AI research velocity
metadata.description: Per-institution velocity layer over the free /api/research/institutions/ai 365-day OpenAlex baseline. Joins it with a fresh 30-day OpenAlex group_by query (cached 24h) and computes velocity_ratio = annualized-30d-output / 365d-baseline. Returns direction classification (accelerating / steady / decelerating / insufficient_data), top-5 notable_movers in each direction, plus by-country and by-type breakdowns. CC0 underlying data; compute is the gate.

metadata.name: PyPI packages momentum
metadata.description: Adds a momentum layer over the free /api/packages/pypi/ai-trending leaderboard: per-package momentum_ratio (last_week vs weekly avg in last_month), velocity_ratio (today annualized vs last_week pace), direction classification (accelerating / steady / decelerating / insufficient_data), top-5 notable movers in each direction, plus by-category counts. Single-snapshot computable; no historical accumulation required.

metadata.name: Economy series full history
metadata.description: Full upstream observation history for any BLS (10y) or FRED series, normalized into one canonical shape across both sources. Each observation paired with its YoY change, 3-month and 12-month moving averages, plus summary min/max and 3-observation trend direction. Free /api/economy/bls/indicators caps at 24 monthly observations and /api/economy/fred/indicators at 90; this is the full archive plus the compute layer.

metadata.name: MCP registry series
metadata.description: Multi-day time series of the official Model Context Protocol server registry: total server count, active/deprecated breakdown, daily added and removed counts. 90-day max range. Compounds daily; backfill is impossible.

metadata.name: LLM probe series
metadata.description: Per-day SLA series for one LLM provider, measured by TensorFeed itself: count, success rate, ttfb p50/p95/p99, total p50/p95/p99, incident-hour count. 90-day max range. Unique because we measure (vs. self-report). Pairs with /api/premium/routing.

metadata.name: Compare models
metadata.description: Side-by-side comparison of 2-5 AI models with pricing, benchmarks normalized to union-of-keys, status, and rankings (cheapest blended, most context, per-benchmark leaderboard).

metadata.name: Provider deep-dive
metadata.description: Everything about one AI provider in one paid call: live status, all models with pricing + tier + benchmark scores joined, recent news, agent traffic. Aggregation over 4 free endpoints.

metadata.name: Cost projection
metadata.description: Project the cost of a token-usage workload across 1-10 AI models. Returns daily/weekly/monthly/yearly totals per model plus a ranking by cheapest monthly.

metadata.name: Webhook watches
metadata.description: Register a webhook watch on a price change or service status transition. HMAC-SHA256 signed POST deliveries. 90-day TTL, default fire cap 100, per-token cap 25.

freeEndpoints.description: Latest AI news articles aggregated from 15+ sources. Free, no auth.
Real-time AI service status (Claude, OpenAI, Gemini, Mistral, Cohere, Replicate, Hugging Face). Free, no auth.
AI model pricing, context windows, capabilities. Free, no auth.
AI model benchmark scores. Free, no auth.
Daily historical snapshots of pricing, models, benchmarks, status, and agent activity. Free, no auth. Single-day raw payload; aggregated views are premium.
Free top-1 routing recommendation. Rate-limited to 5 calls per UTC day per IP. Premium /api/premium/routing has no rate limit and returns top-N with full breakdown.
Real-time AI agent traffic metrics on tensorfeed.ai. Free, no auth.
Aggregated GPU rental pricing across cloud GPU marketplaces (Vast.ai + RunPod). Cheapest on-demand and spot per canonical GPU. Refreshed every 4 hours. Free, no auth.
Top 3 cheapest current GPU offers for one canonical GPU class. Params: ?gpu=H100&type=on_demand|spot. Agent-friendly entry point. Free, no auth.
